rulings.Case Title: SANTIAGO ROBLETO VS. JULIA VARGAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Re Reconsideration Of Ruling On Plaintiffs Robleto'S Motion To Quash Subpoena For Consumer Records, Etc. [Per Order Filed Aug-23-2013]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday September 16, 2013 Line 1 - Hearing Re Reconsideration Of Ruling On Plaintiffs Robleto'S Motion To Quash Subpoena For Consumer Records, Etc. [Per Order Filed Aug-23-2013] Pro Tem Judge Phil Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Hearing required. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. WHITE SNOW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 16, 2013, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-Defendant Prince of Peace Enterprises' demurrer is sustained. The demurrer to the twelfth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. The expiration of the statute of limitations is not a cause of action. The demurrer to causes of action one through eleven are sustained with leave to amend for failure to allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action (CCP §430.10(e)). The Cross-Complaint, liberally construed, has not set forth the elements of a cause of action under any theory. As to the 1st and 3rd causes of action, Cross-Complainant Martin Eng ("Mr. Eng") has not plead facts sufficient to establish the existence of a contract. As to the 2nd cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish that Cross-Defendant was involved in business or that Cross-Defendant caused injury to consumers. As to the 4th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish a relationship between the parties or to establish there are uncertain sums relating to damages. As to the 5th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish there was an expectation of compensation. As to the 6th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish reasonable or foreseeable reliance on a promise. As to the 7th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish that Cross-Defendant had a legal duty to Mr. Eng. As to the 8th cause of action, Cross-Complainant has not plead facts sufficient to establish Cross-Defendant made a representation with the intent to have Mr. Eng rely, that Mr. Eng actually relied, and Mr. Eng was injured as a result of that reliance. As to the 9th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish goods were purchased from Cross-Defendant. As to the 10th cause of action, Cross-Complainant has not plead with sufficient particularity, as a fraud cause of action requires the complaint to "show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered." (Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73.) As to the 11th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish Cross-Defendant intended to inflict emotional distress or that Mr. Eng was present at the time the conduct occurred. Cross-Complainant is granted 20 days leave to amend from notice of entry of order, if he can do so in good faith, to plead facts sufficient to state a cause of action against Cross-Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JIANJUN XIE VS. VICENTE NAVARRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 16, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT VICENTE NAVARRO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND The Court continues the hearing based on the document filed by Plaintiff on September 13, 2013 entitled "Request for Defendant's Matter Off Calendar." Plaintiff represents himself and states he is unavailable until October 30, 2013. The hearing will be held on October 30, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. Any opposition and reply briefs must be filed and served in accordance with the Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b). In light of Plaintiff's requested continuance of this hearing, the Court orders that Plaintiff shall NOT execute on the default judgment until the Court rules on Defendant's pending Motion to Set Aside Default Judgment. Further, Plaintiff is directed to San Francisco Local Rule 8.2 B.1. regarding communications with the Court regarding continuances. That rule provides in part that "If the request is to continue the motion to a later date, the request must state that all parties affected by the motion have agreed to the new date." Otherwise, a party must make a motion or an ex parte application for a continuance, and provide notice to the other parties. See California Rules of Court 3.1200-3.1207 regarding the requirements for ex parte applications, including giving notice to the other side. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Defendant's counsel is requested to prepare a proposed Order that restates this tentative ruling and bring the proposed Order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: INGHAM, ERICA ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ALL OTHERS VS. CLUB ONE, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 16, 2013, Line 10, PLAINTIFF INGHAM, ERICA ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ALL OTHERS MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint Granted, in part. Within 10 days, Plaintiff may file and serve her Second Amended Complaint. The response to admission by Defendant Club One demonstrates that Defendants may have terminated Plaintiff in retaliation for her request for a pregnancy accommodation. California has a strong policy in favor of granting leave to amend that dictates Plaintiff should be permitted to plead her new cause of action. Discovery is reopened for the limited purpose of permitting all parties to conduct discovery as to the new cause of action. Defendants' request to shorten the statutory notice time on a motion for summary adjudication is denied; the Court may not do this without all parties' consent. CCP 437c(a) . Plaintiff's request for the Second Amended Complaint be "deemed" filed and served is denied. Plaintiff must actually file and serve her Second Amended Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TERRY MCCOY et al VS. BALWANTSINH THAKOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-13-532668 WITH CASE CGC-12-520686
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 16, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANTS BALWANTSINH THAKOR, LATABEN THAKOR, SHREE JALARAM HOTEL LP'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-13-532668 WITH CASE CGC-12-520686 The motion is denied. The motion is untimely in light of the November 18, 2013 trial date in case CGC-12-520686. The other case (CGC-13-532668) was just filed on July 8, 2013. While the cases share common legal issues, the overlap in factual issues is more attenuated. The tenants resided at the hotel at different times. While there may be some overlap in what defects were encountered, each tenant did not have an identical experience. Adding the additional plaintiffs in the later filed case to this case--where there are already 25 plaintiffs--will complicate the case and may lead to juror confusion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LANCE FARRELL VS. RYSIGO TECHNOLOGIES CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rysigo Technologies Corporation Motion To Compel Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday September 16, 2013 Line 4 Part 1 - Defendant Rysigo Technologies Corporation's Motion To Compel Deposition. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion to Compel Deposition is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff Lance Farrell and his attorney, Ashwin Ladva, agreed to the deposition dates of August 8, 2013 starting at 10:00 a.m. and to be completed the next day on August 9, 2013 starting at 12:00 p.m. The deposition was to be held in San Francisco. The plaintiff lived in New Mexico at the time the deposition was noticed and would need to plan to take time off work on that Thursday and Friday and would need to make travel plans to fly from New Mexico to San Francisco. This was the second time his deposition had been noticed. The first time it was set for June 10, 2013. It was changed by Mr. Ladva because he needed to visit his ill mother in London. Mr. Ladva agreed to present his client for deposition on August 8th and 9th. A new notice was sent out for those dates which included a request for production of documents. Less than 24 hours before the deposition, Mr. Ladva cancelled the deposition. The reasons stated in Mr. Ladva's opposition are that his client informed him on or about August 5, 2013 that he was "unable to make his deposition date due to work activities". There was no declaration from Mr. Farrell stating what those work activities were, why they came up unexpectedly a few days before the deposition, why they could not be changed or postponed or what efforts he had made to secure and then cancel a flight prior to the deposition. Absent that type of evidence from Mr. Farrell, there is no substantial justification for cancelling the deposition less than 24 hours of the agreed upon and properly noticed deposition. There is also no substantial justification for Mr. Ladva waiting from August 5th until August 7th to notify Mr. Carter that his client would not be able to attend the deposition. No objections were made to the notice of deposition which required the production of documents at the deposition. Mr. Suhail Maqsood of Rysigo signed a declaration stating that he lost $5,600 in business revenue because he cut his business trip short in Chicago and had he known the deposition was off sooner, he would have been able to earn that income by staying in Chicago and meeting his business obligations. He is the a defendant in this case and planned to be present during the deposition of Mr. Farrell. His declaration, did not, however, state that the income was irreparably lost and that he was not able to earn that income at a later date. Mr. Carter states he has spent 7.5 hours on this motion to date at $350.00 per hour for a total of $2,625 plus filing fees of $197.90 for a total of $2,822.90. Based on the forgoing, the tentative ruling is as follows: 1. Lance Farrell is ordered to appear at his deposition on September 19, 2013 and September 20, 2013. 2. Objections to the request to produce documents at the deposition have been waived and all documents are to be produced by hand no later than 30 minutes before the commencement of the deposition on September 19, 2013. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLE NOLLETTE VS. LRICO SERVICES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 16, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANTS LRICO SERVICES, LLC AND DISTILLERY NO. 209 LTD. NAPA, CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is overruled as to all causes of action. Plaintiff's allegations are accepted as true in ruling on a demurrer. (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal. App. 3d 593, 604.) As to the first cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that she was discharged in violation of Labor Code 216. (See FAC 23-24.) As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to meet the heightened pleading standards of fraud, and that she justifiably relied on Defendants' misrepresentation that she would be an owner of the company. (See FAC 9, 28-32.) As to the third cause of action, Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts that an oral promise was made by agents of Defendants that she would be an owner of the company, and that Defendants breached the oral promise by not making her an owner of the company. (See FAC 9, 16.) As to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to show that agents of Defendants intentionally interfered with the benefits of the oral promise to make her an owner of the company. (FAC 43-45.) As to the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts showing Defendants made a promise, and that she relied on to her detriment by leaving her previous job which offered high compensation. (See FAC 9, 49-50.) The Court does not consider grounds for demurrer raised for the first time in reply. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SCOTT SMITH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Slapp Motion; Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 16, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANT SCOTT SMITH Slapp Motion; The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is denied. Defendant fails to show that plaintiff's complaint is based on petitioning or other First Amendment activities. Defendant has not carried his burden with regard to prong one of CCP 425.16. The gravamen of plaintiff's suit is to unwind an allegedly fraudulent transfer. The allegations in paragraphs 10 and 13 in the Complaint are incidental and not a basis for liability. See Martinez v. Metabolife Intern., Inc., (2003) 113 Cal.App.4th 181, 188. The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
